WARNING: This data is ONLY for CSE and CS branch.
course_objectives: To provide students with a foundational understanding of AI concepts, tools, and practical implementations, including search algorithms, knowledge representation, and ethical considerations.
To equip students with hands-on experience in designing and implementing agentic AI systems, genetic algorithms, and their real-world applications.
course_outcomes: CO1: Demonstrate foundational understanding of AI, implementing logic-based systems and classical search algorithms.
CO2: Design and simulate intelligent agents and multi-agent systems in diverse environments.
CO3: Apply reinforcement learning techniques to develop learning agents and multi-agent environments.
CO4: Construct and optimize Genetic Algorithm-based solutions to solve complex real-world problems.
CO5: Evaluate and integrate AI techniques for real-world applications.

units.title: Practical Foundations of Artificial Intelligence
topics: Definition, History, and Evolution of AI
Key Domains: Machine Learning, Deep Learning, NLP, Computer Vision
AI Applications in Healthcare, Finance, and Robotics
Python for AI: NumPy, Pandas, and Scikit-learn
Informed Search Strategies: A*, Greedy Best-First Search
Developing a Maze Solver using Search Algorithms
Implementing A* Algorithm for Path Planning
Responsible AI Practices, Fairness, Transparency, and Explainability
Implementing Logic-Based Systems with Python Libraries
mini_projects: Implement a Tic-Tac-Toe AI using Minimax Algorithm
Create a Pathfinding Visualizer for BFS/DFS/A*

units.title: Foundations of Agentic AI and Intelligent Agents
topics: Types of Agents: Reflex, Goal-Based, Utility-Based
Agent Environments: Fully vs. Partially Observable
Build Reflex and Goal-Based Agents in Python
Self-Navigating Agent in a Virtual Grid
Real-World Applications: Smart Grids, Collaborative Robot
Basics of Game Theory and Nash Equilibrium
units.mini_project: Build a Reflex Agent for Maze Navigation

units.title: Learning Agents and Practical Agentic AI Systems
topics: Develop AI Agent for Snake or Flappy Bird
Traffic Control Simulation using Multi-Agent Systems
units.mini_project: Solve Frozen Lake Environment using Q-Learning

units.title: Genetic Algorithms & Applications
topics: Writing a Basic GA from Scratch in Python
Hybrid GAs: Integration with Local Search and Simulated Annealing
units.mini_project: Solve Optimal Timetable Scheduling for a University

textbooks: Manaranjan Pradhan & U. Dinesh Kumar (Wiley India)
S. Russell and P. Norvig - Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach, Prentice Hall, Third Edition, 2009
I. Bratko - Prolog: Programming for Artificial Intelligence, Fourth edition, Addison-Wesley
M. Tim Jones - Artificial Intelligence: A Systems Approach (Computer Science), Jones and Bartlett, 2008
Nils J. Nilsson - The Quest for Artificial Intelligence, Cambridge University Press, 2009
Gerhard Weiss - Multi Agent Systems, Second Edition, MIT Press, 2013
David L. Poole and Alan K. Mackworth - Artificial Intelligence: Foundations of Computational Agents, Cambridge University Press, 2010
Online Resource: NPTEL's free AI Course by Prof. Deepak Khemani, IIT Madras
reference_books: Artificial Intelligence with Python - Prateek Joshi (Indian Author, Packt Publishing)
